phase_1.audio: Audio recording captures initial spike of 98.3 dBA, gradually settling to 72.3-79.3 dBA midway through session. One or more speech segments identified (avg confidence 0.88), suggesting social environment. Higher ambient noise detected; concentration requires active focus effort.
phase_1.vitals: Vitals monitoring recorded 71 bpm as session average. This aligns with ideal focus-state parameters.
phase_1.gps_motion: GPS signal strength inconsistent at start (5.4m error margin), achieving 1.7m lock after stabilization. Accelerometer readings show user quickly becoming immobile and remaining seated throughout.

phase_2.correlations: Correlation analysis reveals moderate compatibility between positional, kinetic, and acoustic factors.
phase_2.holistic_assessment: Moderate focus conditions evident: audio levels at 79.3 dB, stable user state. Session duration (38s) limits comprehensive concentration evaluation.

phase_3.recommendations: Invest in noise-canceling technology to mitigate high ambient levels. Current 38-second duration too brief for meaningful concentration assessment; aim for 10+ min sessions.

reason: Score weighted toward audio environment (79.3dB—loud), stable positioning, and session duration (38s). Overall favorable conditions.
